member.name: T:gdio.common.objects.CoordinateConversion
member.summary: Enumeration for the type of coordinate conversion.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.CoordinateConversion.None
member.summary: None returns the gameObject.transform.position without any conversion

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.CoordinateConversion.Local
member.summary: Local returns the gameObject.transform.localposition without any conversion

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.CoordinateConversion.WorldToScreenPoint
member.summary: WorldToScreenPoint returns the coordinates of the object transformed from World-To-Screen by the specified Camera. The default is Camera.main.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.CoordinateConversion.WorldToViewportPoint
member.summary: WorldToViewPortPoint returns the coordinates of the object in the screen space of the specified Camera. The default is Camera.main.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.CoordinateConversion.ScreenToWorldPoint
member.summary: ScreenToWorldPoint returns the coordinates of the object in the world space of the specified Camera. The default is Camera.main.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.CoordinateConversion.ScreenToViewportPoint
member.summary: ScreenToViewPortPoint returns the coordinates of the object in the viewport space of the specified Camera. The default is Camera.main.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.CoordinateConversion.ViewportToWorldPoint
member.summary: ScreenToWorldPoint returns the coordinates of the object in the world space of the specified Camera. The default is Camera.main.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.CoordinateConversion.ViewportToScreenPoint
member.summary: ScreenToViewPortPoint returns the coordinates of the object in the viewport space of the specified Camera. The default is Camera.main.

member.name: T:gdio.common.objects.Quaternion
member.summary: A representation of 3D rotation. See for more information.
member.remarks: Under UNREALENGINE, Euler(pitch, yaw, roll) maps to Unreal's FRotator (Z-up, ZYX extrinsic order). eulerAngles returns (Pitch, Yaw, Roll) matching FQuat::Rotator(). Under Unity, Euler(x, y, z) uses YXZ intrinsic order (Y-up). The identity quaternion is (0, 0, 0, 1).
member.param: The x vector of the rotation.
The y vector of the rotation.
The z vector of the rotation.
A scalar that stores the rotation around the vector.

member.summary: The w component.

member.summary: The x component.

member.summary: The y component.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Quaternion.z
member.summary: The z component.

member.name: T:gdio.common.objects.Matrix4x4
member.summary: A standard 4x4 transformation matrix.
member.remarks: Under UNREALENGINE, uses row-major convention: vectors are row vectors multiplied on the left (v * M). Translation is stored in the last row (M[3][0..2]). Matrix multiplication order is left-to-right (A then B = A*B). Under Unity, uses column-major convention: vectors are column vectors (M * v). Translation is in the last column. Computed properties (inverse, transpose, determinant, isIdentity, lossyScale, rotation, decomposeProjection) are marked [IgnoreMember] and are never serialized — use ComputeInverse() / ComputeTranspose() / ComputeDeterminant() in serialized contexts.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Matrix4x4.decomposeProjection
member.summary: Decomposes a projection matrix into near/far plane distances and field-of-view values (read-only, not serialized).

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Matrix4x4.determinant
member.summary: The determinant of the matrix (read-only, not serialized). Use ComputeDeterminant() in test assertions.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Matrix4x4.inverse
member.summary: The inverse of this matrix (read-only, not serialized). Use ComputeInverse() in test assertions.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Matrix4x4.isIdentity
member.summary: Returns true if this matrix is the identity matrix (read-only, not serialized).

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Matrix4x4.lossyScale
member.summary: The scale component extracted from this matrix (read-only, not serialized, approximation for non-uniform scales).

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Matrix4x4.rotation
member.summary: The rotation component extracted from this matrix as a Quaternion (read-only, not serialized).

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Matrix4x4.transpose
member.summary: The transpose of this matrix (read-only, not serialized). Use ComputeTranspose() in test assertions.

member.returns: Point transformed to world space (includes translation).

member.summary: Transforms direction by this matrix ignoring translation, using Unreal row-vector convention.

member.name: M:gdio.common.objects.Transform.TransformPoint(gdio.common.objects.Vector3)
member.summary: Transforms from local space to world space (includes translation and scale). Unreal equivalent: FTransform::TransformPosition.

member.name: M:gdio.common.objects.Transform.InverseTransformPoint(gdio.common.objects.Vector3)
member.summary: Transforms from world space to local space (includes translation and scale). Unreal equivalent: FTransform::InverseTransformPosition.

member.name: M:gdio.common.objects.Transform.TransformDirection(gdio.common.objects.Vector3)

member.name: M:gdio.common.objects.Transform.InverseTransformDirection(gdio.common.objects.Vector3)
member.summary: Transforms from world space to local space, rotation only (not affected by scale or position). Unreal equivalent: FTransform::InverseTransformVectorNoScale.

member.name: M:gdio.common.objects.Transform.TransformVector(gdio.common.objects.Vector3)

member.name: M:gdio.common.objects.Transform.InverseTransformVector(gdio.common.objects.Vector3)
member.summary: Transforms from world space to local space (affected by scale; no translation). Unreal equivalent: FTransform::InverseTransformVector.

member.name: T:gdio.common.objects.LogLevel
member.summary: Logging levels used to control Agent-side logging verbosity. Used with api.SetLoggingLevel() and api.LogMessageOnAgent().
member.remarks: Higher numeric values indicate more verbose logging. Messages are only logged if their level is less than or equal to the currently set logging level on the Agent.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.LogLevel.DISABLED
member.summary: Disables all logging output from the Agent.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.LogLevel.ERROR
member.summary: Logs only critical errors that prevent normal operation.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.LogLevel.WARN
member.summary: Logs warnings and errors. Use for potential issues that don't halt execution.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.LogLevel.INFO
member.summary: Logs informational messages, warnings, and errors. General operational information.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.LogLevel.DEBUG
member.summary: Logs detailed debugging information along with info, warnings, and errors.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.LogLevel.TRACE
member.summary: Most verbose logging level. Logs all messages including detailed trace information.

member.name: T:gdio.common.objects.MethodInfo
member.summary: Contains metadata about a method Provides method signature information including name, return type and parameters
member.remarks: Used to inspect available methods on game objects before invoking them with api.CallMethod(). Returned as part of a HashSet from GetMethodList() in both Unity and Unreal engines.

member.name: P:gdio.common.objects.MethodInfo.MethodName
member.summary: The name of the method (e.g., "TakeDamage", "SetActive").

member.name: P:gdio.common.objects.MethodInfo.ReturnTypeFullName
member.summary: The fully qualified return type name (e.g., "System.Void", "System.Single", "UnityEngine.GameObject").

member.name: P:gdio.common.objects.MethodInfo.ArgumentNames
member.summary: Collection of parameter names in declaration order (e.g., ["amount", "damageType"]).

member.name: P:gdio.common.objects.MethodInfo.ArgumentTypeFullNames
member.summary: Collection of fully qualified parameter type names in declaration order (e.g., ["System.Single", "System.String"]).

member.name: P:gdio.common.objects.MethodInfo.ParentHierarchyPath
member.summary: The HierarchyPath to the object or component where this method is declared. Use this path when calling the method with api.CallMethod().

member.name: T:gdio.common.objects.PropertyInfo
member.summary: Represents property information for a game object retrieved via GameDriver API. Used by GetPropertyList to return properties and their values.

member.name: P:gdio.common.objects.PropertyInfo.TypeFullName
member.summary: The full type name of the property (e.g., "System.Boolean", "gdio.common.objects.Vector3")

member.name: P:gdio.common.objects.PropertyInfo.HierarchyPath
member.summary: The hierarchy path to this property in the object tree

member.name: P:gdio.common.objects.PropertyInfo.DisplayValue
member.summary: The property's display value as a human-readable string representation

member.name: P:gdio.common.objects.PropertyInfo.Value
member.summary: The property value encoded as a byte array, to be autodecoded based on TypeFullName

member.name: P:gdio.common.objects.PropertyInfo.Children
member.summary: Sub-fields of this property when it is a struct/value type. Null for primitives, strings, enums, and class references. Populated by GetPropertyTree up to the requested depth.

member.name: P:gdio.common.objects.PropertyInfo.IsObjectRef
member.summary: True when this property is a class/object reference that can be explored further by calling GetPropertyTree with this node's HierarchyPath. Structs and primitives are never flagged; class types are flagged regardless of whether they have a ToGDIO serialization (which determines whether DisplayValue is populated).

member.summary: THe HierarchyPath of the GameObject that recieved the OnCollision event

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Hit.Timestamp
member.summary: The timestamp of when the collision occured

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Hit.CollisionType
member.summary: The enumeration value of the collision type

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Hit.Location
member.summary: The location in world space where the moving shape would end up against the impacted object, if there is a hit.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Hit.Normal
member.summary: Normal of the hit in world space, for the object that was swept.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Hit.gameObject
member.summary: The LiteGameObject of the collision,

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Hit.BlockingHit
member.summary: PrimitiveComponent hit by the trace.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.Hit.PenetrationDepth
member.summary: If this test started in penetration (bStartPenetrating is true) and a depenetration vector can be computed, this value is the distance along Normal that will result in moving out of penetration.

member.name: T:gdio.common.objects.FGeometry
member.summary: Represents Unreal Engine's FGeometry structure for UWidget layout information. Retrieved via api.GetObjectFieldValue() when querying widget geometry properties.
member.remarks: Provides position, size, and scale data for Unreal UMG widgets. All position and size values are in slate units.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.FGeometry.AbsolutePosition
member.summary: Absolute screen-space position of the widget in slate units.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.FGeometry.AbsoluteSize
member.summary: Absolute screen-space size of the widget in slate units.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.FGeometry.LocalSize
member.summary: Local-space size of the widget before scale is applied.

member.name: F:gdio.common.objects.FGeometry.Scale
member.summary: DPI scale factor applied to the widget.

member.name: T:gdio.common.lookup.HPathObject
member.summary: Wrapper class that allows HierarchyPath strings to be passed as method arguments to game methods via api.CallMethod(). The Agent automatically resolves the HPath to the actual game object when the method is invoked.
member.remarks: Use HPathObject when a game method expects a GameObject/UObject reference as a parameter. This eliminates the need for custom serialization when passing object references between test code and the game.

member.name: F:gdio.common.lookup.HPathObject.HierarchyPath
member.summary: The HierarchyPath that will be resolved to a game object when passed to a method.

member.name: M:gdio.common.lookup.HPathObject.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new HPathObject wrapper around a HierarchyPath string.
member.param: The HierarchyPath to the game object that should be passed as a method argument.

member.name: M:gdio.common.lookup.HPathObject.ToString
member.summary: Returns the HierarchyPath as a string.

member.name: T:gdio.common.lookup.ObjectRef
member.summary: Represents a reference handle to an object object stored in the GameDriver Agent. ObjectRefs allow you to maintain persistent references to objects across multiple API calls.
member.remarks:
ObjectRefs are created using api.CreateObjectRef() and can be used in two ways: 1. Directly passed to API methods like api.GetObjectFieldValue(objectRef, "fieldName") 2. Within HPath queries using the fn:objectref() function: fn:objectref('{objectRef}')/@propertyName
The actual object reference is stored on the Agent side, and the ObjectRef acts as a handle. ObjectRefs should be removed with api.RemoveObjectRef() when no longer needed to free Agent-side resources.

member.name: F:gdio.common.lookup.ObjectRef.Identifier
member.summary: Unique identifier for this object reference. This is the handle used to look up the actual object on the Agent side. Generated automatically when the ObjectRef is created.

member.name: F:gdio.common.lookup.ObjectRef.HPath
member.summary: The HierarchyPath that was used to create this ObjectRef (if created via HPath). Empty if the ObjectRef was created by instantiating a new object via Type or type name.

member.name: M:gdio.common.lookup.ObjectRef.ToString
member.summary: Returns the Identifier as a string, allowing the ObjectRef to be easily embedded in HPath queries and other string contexts.
